goal: Run a belt-speed what-if on CV_04 (0.8 → 1.2 m/s), spawn boxes at a periodic cadence, and honestly frame Isaac Sim as visual validation, not FlexSim.
thoughts: This is kinematic + rigid-body, not RL — never reach for launch_training. create_conveyor exposes the real belt-speed attribute; modify it. Periodic box spawn is plain Python on the update-event stream, no fake queue-sim tool. Honest about Isaac Sim vs FlexSim: Isaac gives visual + collision sanity, FlexSim gives distributional throughput. Parameterize for 1.0, 1.2, 1.4 m/s trials.
code:
# Kinematic what-if, not a discrete-event sim.

# 1. Find CV_04 and the belt-speed attribute name
list_all_prims(stage_path="<DC_STAGE>", name_filter="CV_04")

# 2. Edit belt speed (parameterized for sweep)
for speed in [<0.8>, <1.0>, <1.2>, <1.4>]:
    create_conveyor(
        prim_path="/World/Conveyors/CV_04",
        belt_speed=speed,
        update_in_place=True
    )
    # record approx clear-time per speed (see spawner below)

# 3. Periodic box spawn (plain Python, no fake queue-sim extension)
run_usd_script(script="""
import omni.kit.app
from pxr import UsdGeom, Gf, Sdf
import omni.usd
stage = omni.usd.get_context().get_stage()

SPAWN_EVERY_S = <1.5>
PALLET_REF    = "<PALLET_USD_PATH>"
SPAWN_POS     = (<X>, <Y>, <Z>)

t = [0.0]; i = [0]
def on_update(e):
    t[0] += e.payload["dt"]
    if t[0] >= SPAWN_EVERY_S:
        t[0] = 0.0
        p = f"/World/SpawnedBoxes/box_{i[0]:04d}"; i[0] += 1
        stage.DefinePrim(p, "Xform").GetReferences().AddReference(PALLET_REF)
        UsdGeom.XformCommonAPI(stage.GetPrimAtPath(p)).SetTranslate(SPAWN_POS)
_sub = omni.kit.app.get_app().get_update_event_stream().create_subscription_to_pop(on_update)
""")

# 4. Post-run diagnose
run_stage_analysis(stage_path="<DC_STAGE>")

# Tell Fatima:
#   - Rough boxes/hour you can read off visual clearance time: "fuzzy",
#     +/- 20%. If you need 2-decimal throughput, FlexSim/AnyLogic is the
#     better tool. Isaac Sim is for visual + collision sanity + the demo.
#   - Yes, FlexSim would do the pure-throughput question better.
#   - The sweep is parameterized (0.8, 1.0, 1.2, 1.4) — swap the list.
failure_modes: Reaching for launch_training / RL for a kinematic problem
Pretending Isaac Sim gives FlexSim-grade throughput numbers
Inventing a queue-sim extension instead of a plain Python spawner
Hardcoding one speed instead of parameterizing for the sweep
